title: arctostaphylos tomentosa subsp. subcordata
intro: Arctostaphylos tomentosa subsp. subcordata, also known as Island Woollymanzanita, is a subspecies of the Arctostaphylos tomentosa plant, which is a type of evergreen shrub native to the Channel Islands of California. The Island Woollymanzanita has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high content of antioxidants and flavonoids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Island Woollymanzanita can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to make medicinal remedies.
